creator: mav@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 28.07.2012 10:50, Steve McCoy wrote:
>   Sorry for the delay. Here are my test results for r238658, first for the "bonnie" benchmark:
>
>                  ------------Sequential Output------------ ------Sequential Input----- ----Random---
>                  --Per Char-- -----Block---- ---Rewrite--  --Per Char-- -----Block---- ----Seeks----
>   Machine    MB  K/sec  %CPU  K/sec    %CPU  K/sec  %CPU  K/sec   %CPU   K/sec   %CPU   /sec   %CPU
>
>   209896    100 141372 99.66 176170   54.72 226918 62.42 153879 100.04 3551889  98.74 275536 232.08
>   209897    100  99306 99.78  89651.8 49.82 110016 74.5  152890  99.52 4443177  95.72 171110 181.42
>     +mwait   100 177231 99.98 364950   66.14 423017 66.2  153827  99.26 3288740 103.1  279108 240.38
>   238658    100 114366 99.7  127046   71.4  142934 88.4  153747  99.86 3225140 101.32 316054 256.08
>
>   Seems like some mixed results, but most speeds are at least better than r209897.
>   Here are the transactions per second from pgbench:
>
>   209896:  3390.540503
>   209897:  2724.740134
>     +mwait: 3883.045736
>   238658:  3632.487937
>
>   Not as good as with mwait, but better than r209896.

mwait result is probably very hard to beat from performance point. 
Though you could try this patch to hopefully get a bit closer:
http://people.freebsd.org/~mav/sleep_time.patch (for HEAD)
http://people.freebsd.org/~mav/sleep_time_8.patch (for 8-STABLE)

The patch switches ACPI idle code from using ACPI time counter to the 
timer counter used by the rest of the system. With 8-STABLE I am not 
sure you have fastest TSC time counter, but even HPET should be faster 
then ACPI on modern systems.

-- 
Alexander Motin
